hypothesis: Augmenting the ∆-learning XGBoost model with metal-centered ligand-field descriptors (octahedral/tetrahedral geometry indices, M–L bond-length and bond-angle dispersion) and xTB-derived local spin moments and d-occupancies will reduce the HSE06 band-gap MAE for MOFs containing Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, or Ni by at least 30% relative to the published xTB-∆-XGBoost baseline on identical train/test splits, while altering the MAE for non–3d-metal MOFs by no more than 0.02 eV.
